title: Seamless, Wild, and Worth Every Moment
text:
We did a 2 day / 3 night camping trip with Kalahari Tours in Chobe National Park, and it was an incredible experience from start to finish.
We were a group of four without our own vehicle, and everything was handled seamlessly, including transfers from Victoria Falls and drop-off in Livingstone. Super easy and stress-free.
The trip itself was action-packed from the moment we arrived. Great game drives, an excellent boat cruise, and amazing sightings throughout. Camping in the park was a real highlight, it felt like a proper immersion in the wild, but we always felt safe with such an experienced team.
Evenings around the fire under a sky full of stars made it extra special.
Big credit to the whole team, and especially our guide, Mr T. His knowledge and tracking skills made a huge difference, we saw a pride of lions in motion and even three different leopards in three days.
Absolutely worth every Pula and highly recommended.

title: 100% recommended
text: This is 100% the tour to book if you are in Victoria Falls, Livingstone or Kasane.  Our initial enquiries to Kirstin and Lunza were dealt with promptly and they were extremely flexible with my non stop questions!  We were picked up in Vic Falls by Joseph, he was so interesting and welcoming and we made it across the border with ease to Mr T, our guide for the safari.  The morning was spent with another awesome guide (name escapes me) on the Chobe River with hippos, crocs and birds.  A gorgeous lunch at Cafe Buzz where the office is located meant we could drop any unrequired bags.  Mr T was amazing, his humour kept us amused and despite having had a 5 hour rainstorm the day before making wildlife spotting more challenging, his determination was something to behold.  Our camp for the night was well set up, great team there with fab food.  Mr T kept going the next day and we were rewarded over the 2 days with the 'Big 5'.  We cannot thank you all enough for such a warm welcome and wonderful safari.  Do not hesitate to book this tour.  Highly recommended.

text:
We had the most amazing experience with Kalahari Tours in Botswana! Our guide, Kennedy, was absolutely exceptional, knowledgeable, funny, kind, and always ready to help with everything. Kennedy went above and beyond in every possible way , from spotting animals we would never have seen on our own to making us feel completely safe and welcome throughout the journey. He’s not just a great guide, but a truly wonderful person.

We feel so lucky to have met him and would 100% recommend Kalahari Tours (and Kennedy!) to anyone who wants a genuine, memorable safari experience.

Thank you Ken!

Jo and Jojo

title: Unforgettable Chobe Safari Experience!!
text:
Our Chobe safari was absolutely incredible from start to finish! Our guide, Six, was truly exceptional — so knowledgeable, patient, and passionate about the wildlife. He went above and beyond to make sure we saw as many animals as possible, from lions and elephants to hippos and rare birds. We always felt safe and comfortable in his care, and his love for nature made every moment even more special.

A huge thank you as well to Chef Pete, who spoiled us with the most delicious meals! Every dish was made with care and flavor — the perfect way to refuel after our adventures. His warmth and hospitality made the experience feel like home in the middle of the bush.

The entire team made this trip unforgettable. If you’re thinking about a safari in Chobe — don’t hesitate, just go! You’ll be in the best hands with Six and Chef Pete.

text: We couldn’t have asked for a better trip. Our guide, Six, was truly the best. He was so knowledgeable about the animals, their habits, and how to locate them. title: Botswana Bush, Boats & Beasts
text:
Lonely Planet was impetus for inquiry at Kalahari - the idea of bush camp was intriguing, and the commentary that guides were more knowledgable than most hooked us. Reaching out via email, Lunza responded immediately and was  helpful and professional and made inquiry/booking easy. Fun to finally meet her!
Turns out LP was spot on - sleeping IN Chobe NP  is a unique experience in many ways. We drifted off to lions' roars and woke to hippo snuffles, took in the southern stars around campfire, and noshed on fabulous vegetarian & meat-based fare and MP's homemade bread. Dinners included fine enough SA red/white wine, and the bush snax and tea service were inventive and welcome!  Tents were sturdy and mobile  shower/toilet were more than adequate. 

The show stopper is, of course, the animals, birds and landscapes. Moses, our seasoned and dedicated guide, was uncannily able to make every game drive/river cruise a combo of storytelling, surprises and stellar sightings. He group up on these waters and velds, and it shows.  The best bit was the solo sunset access to wildlife-we didn't have a far drive at day's end to our bush camp, and as such  had private dusk sightings (including close up leopard with two cubs and pride of lionesses with 7 cubs standing off against two encroaching males). 

We've traveled all over the world, primarily for authentic animal and cultural  experiences, and this is as good as it gets. Ask for Moses.

ownerResponse.text: Dear Lisa thanks for the well written report ! We are thrilled you enjoyed your time with us and Mp's culinary skills as well as Moses guiding you . Please spred the word and do join us again one day !

Reaching out via email, Lunza responded immediately and was  helpful and professional and made inquiry/booking easy. Fun to finally meet her!
Turns out LP was spot on - sleeping IN Chobe NP  is a unique experience in many ways. We drifted off to lions' roars and woke to hippo snuffles, took in the southern stars around campfire, and noshed on fabulous vegetarian & meat-based fare and MP's homemade bread. Dinners included fine enough SA red/white wine, and the bush snax and tea service were inventive and welcome!  Tents were sturdy and mobile  shower/toilet were more than adequate. 

The show stopper is, of course, the animals, birds and landscapes. Moses, our seasoned and dedicated guide, was uncannily able to make every game drive/river cruise a combo of storytelling, surprises and stellar sightings. He group up on these waters and velds, and it shows.  The best bit was the solo sunset access to wildlife-we didn't have a far drive at day's end to our bush camp, and as such  had private dusk sightings (including close up leopard with two cubs and pride of lionesses with 7 cubs standing off against two encroaching males). 

We've traveled all over the world, primarily for authentic animal and cultural  experiences, and this is as good as it gets. Ask for Moses.

title: You can see all kinds of animals by the water.
text:
To cross the border from Zimbabwe to Botswana, I got off the bus that came from Zimbabwe, entered Botswana and switched to a four-wheel drive.
I could see countless elephants, buffalo and so on.
The lions could see loose animals that they could not see, but were not seen at the Fortytown Zoo, such as elephants, buffalo and giraffes. We could also see crocodiles eating buffalo. There was a beautiful blue bird at the border.

title: Incredible safari with excellent guides and great accommodation
text:
I had an amazing time doing the 2 night, 3 day safari with Kalahari Tours. A large group (18) of me and my friends all thoroughly enjoyed the safari itself and we were lucky to see so many animals on the game drives and boat tours, helped by our very well informed guides Gee and Chika. Everyone really enjoyed driving around with Gee and Chika and they made sure to find as many animals as possible and were very knowledgeable telling us about them. The cars are also well stocked with snacks and water and very comfortable. 

We also really enjoyed the camping which was set up by Amos. The site is perfectly set up with great tents, a fire, toilet and shower facilities. The bed itself is a small mattress and pillow and very comfortable. We were offered warm water in a basin outside our tents every morning to wash our face. 

The food made by Dicks was also excellent. There was no shortage of food or water the entire tour with people able to have as much as they wanted. They also accommodated any allergies with no problem. 

Everything about the tour was extremely professional and slick. We would all really recommend the two night/three day tour with Kalahari Tours and thank you very much to Gee, Chika, Amos and Dicks!

title: At least one night camp is a must to get the real Chobi
text:
Incredible Safari!!

We had recently done the Serengeti and Masaai and wondered if we needed to do another safari and glad we went with our gut because Chobi was insane and so different. 

The landscape and the experience is wild and so much wildlife. Would 100% do the camp or stay in Chobi somewhere as all the action came at dawn and dusk(as you would think) and it gives you the time to really enjoy what Chobi provides for you. The camp was amazing. We went with basic camp and it was perfect. Great homey camp food, good stories by the fire, simple tents and listening to the African Lullaby to put you to sleep. The guides make you feel completely safe knowing what is out there somewhere. 

The boat in the morning with Opie was great. He helped us swiftly get through the border and provided a great boat safari, lots of knowledge, safe trip and some cool close up encounters with buffalo, hippo, Crocs and lots of intricate bird life.

Our safari guide, Godfrey, what a guy. Such a passion himself for the park and the wildlife. He saw and heard things you wouldn't even think of as a new comer to the park which allowed us to experience so much. He was also like our own personal Chobi encyclopedia and answered all of our questions above and beyond and the intricacies entwined. For example he was looking at the animal tracks In the morning to determine animal direction and where to go. From his experience the days before he knew where certain herds were. While we were stationary he could determine what were warning calls and used other animals behaviour to determine the direction of "danger" but for us "experience". This lead to seeing hippos, Crocs, all the different antelope and birds, leopards in trees and on the ground, an amazing experience with a lion pride of 3 females and multiple cubs, crazy elephant herd encounters and sunsets and meetings a few big residents like lions and also a new sub adults while also walking home with so much knowledge about how the ecosystem interacts. Even explaining how irregular it was to watch a Zebra trying to help it's mate across the river. Then to explain this indicated the mate was sick or injured and the helping zebra was overly caring given the heard was looking to move on. 

Incredible time and 10/10 would recommend.

ownerResponse.text: Thank you Sean for the fantastic report , we're thrilled you pegged Chobe up there with the Serengeti and the Mara . Op and Godrfrey are stars in our valuable team and thank you for recognising them and applauding their efforts . Do come again

title: The best way of doing a Safari
text:
We visited Chobe to enjoy this wonderful experience. The tents were very clean, the camp was fully equipped with everything we could need, and the meals were both delicious and generous. Our personal guide, Khaleb, welcomed us at the border with Zimbabwe and stayed with us until our return. He made our two days truly unforgettable, allowing us to experience the atmosphere of the bush like a real bushman. We are deeply grateful for his hospitality and friendliness. He tried his best to show us as many animals as possible in the park, successfully guiding us to encounters with lions and leopards.

We would also like to thank Mr. P, the camp’s chef, for preparing excellent Italian pasta, Gee for the camp’s organization, and the entire Kalahari Tours staff for making our stay so special.

ownerResponse.text: Thanks ever so much for the awesome review we are so happy you enjoyed your Safari with us .Chobe often delivers -but not always when it comes to Predators so you were really fortunate to see both Lion and leopard . title: Incredible Experience
text:
I did the 2-night, 3-day camping trip in Chobe with Kalahari Tours, and it was honestly one of the best travel experiences I’ve ever had. Everything was well-organized—from booking and pickup to the river cruise, game drives, and camp setup. You could tell it was all run by people who really know what they’re doing.

What made it truly special, though, was our guide Leo. He wasn’t just knowledgeable about the wildlife and the bush—he had a great sense of humor and really made sure everyone was comfortable and having a good time. Pontso, our camp chef, and Jacob also deserve a lot of credit for keeping us well-fed and taken care of.

The whole experience felt like a perfect mix of wild adventure and comfort. I came away with memories I’ll never forget. If you’re looking for a hassle-free, professional, and genuinely memorable safari, Kalahari Tours is the way to go.

title: Exceptional stay in paradise
text:
I spent three days two nights in the paradise that is Chobe National Park, guided by the fantastic and professional team from Kalahari Tours. Our skillful and sharp guides, Free and Lance, kept us safe and informed every step of the way. The stories shared by them made the entire experience even more fulfilling and memorable. MP, the wonderful chef, ensured we were well fed with delicious meals, and the thoughtful host, Misak, made us feel right at home. Every detail was perfectly executed, making the experience truly exceptional.
We explored the park in an open vehicle, surrounded by awe-inspiring wildlife- leopards, giraffes, elephants, lionesses and many more - all watching us as we passed. Our guides gently reminded us to only step out for designated toilets stops, ensuring our safety while allowing us to fully immerse ourselves in the wild beauty of Chobe. It was more than a tour - it was an unforgettable encounter with nature.

ownerResponse.text: Dear Joanne many many thanks for taking the time to review us and your wonderfull compliments to our team mean the world to us . Once again thank you and please do spread the word and do come and see us again .

title: Great Camping Safari in Chobe.
text: We just came back from a 3 day /2 night camping safari and it was great. Camping in the park is best because you get deeper into the park than day trippers. The 3 hosts really made the trip great. The guide, Lance was very experienced, informative and loves Botswana as much as anybody. Misak organized the camp, Ponso cooked and everything was perfect. Comfortable cots in the tents, great food and a nice welcoming Camp fire when we returned each evening. Ice cold beer and drinks on hand at all times. We saw 13 lions, probably 1000 elephants, hundreds of giraffes and Kudus, many hippos and Cape Buffalo. We'll never forget this trip.

title: Amazing Chobe Safari with a Fantastic Guide!
text:
We had an incredible 3-day, 2-night camping safari in Chobe, and it was an unforgettable experience thanks to Gee, our amazing guide. Gee was incredibly flexible and tailored the schedule based on our preferences. During game drives, he coordinated with other guides to help us spot the Big Five — his dedication and knowledge really made the difference.

**Tour Highlights**
Every day on safari brought new and exciting animal sightings. The thrill of chasing specific animals across the Safari was so much fun! Beyond the wildlife, we truly enjoyed connecting with Gee, the team at Katahari Tours, and the other tour participants. It made the entire trip even more special.

**Itinerary Overview**
- 2 nights, 3 days of camping in Chobe National Park
- Day 1: Pick-up from our hotel in Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe, with smooth support crossing the Botswana border
- Morning river cruise followed by an afternoon game drive
- Day 2 & Day 3: Full days of game drives
- On the afternoon of Day 3, we were assisted again with visas and transferred to our accommodation in Livingstone, Zambia

Highly recommend this tour if you want a well-organized, adventure-filled experience with kind, knowledgeable guides!

title: Once in a Lifetime
text:
AMAZING! INCREDIBLE! MESMERIZING!

While I'm sure any safari in Chobe would have been a once-in-a-lifetime experience, it absolutely exceeded my dreams. I attribute this to our guide, Leo, who made the entire trip unforgettable. Even though we visited during the off-season, the park was quieter, which actually made the experience even more enjoyable. The knowledge Leo shared with us truly set this experience apart from any others. He is, for sure, an animal whisperer. We had friends on separate safaris in Chobe at the same time, and they didn’t have nearly the experience we did.

The wildlife, of course, was spectacular. We were lucky enough to see tons of baby animals during the off-season, which made it extra special during this time of year. Leo was great at tracking all the animals. We even saw the lion king roar as he woke up for the night. 

Camping was very comfortable. The food was great, and there was even a hot shower! Jacob and MP treated us very well back at camp with hot meals and anything we needed.

I highly recommend camping, even if you're not usually a fan. Being able to stay right in the park means you don’t have to leave early, like you do with a day trip, and you get to experience things you wouldn’t otherwise, like hearing the actual Lion King's roar as he wakes up for the night! It was an experience I’ll never forget. I could go on and on about the wildlife, but you'll have to see for yourself when you do a safari with [insert safari company name here].

I’ve done two other safaris but nothing compares to this one. If you are going to Chobe...make sure you go with Leo!

ownerResponse.text: Thanks Jules for the awesome review , we're thrilled you enjoyed it so much and were fortunate to see good game in the green season . We will let the team and Leo know you enjoyed the food and the guiding , please do come again !

title: Come to Chobe
text:
Imagine sitting quietly just a few feet from a pride of lions watching them play and sleep. Then watching a large female lion stalking and attacking a giraffe. This was just one afternoon of the two night, three day tour. Other highlights included being surrounded by a herd of Cape Buffalo, seeing a new born elephant, watching giraffes graze quietly, and seeing rare birds. 

Our guide, Mr T, took care of everything. Over the course of three days we spotted nearly 100 species of birds. His knowledge of birds was extremely helpful. We also enjoyed his knowledge of the ecology of Chobe and the history of the park.

The food was provided by MP and Jacob. It was excellent. They also took care of our every need in camp. 

A brilliant experience.

ownerResponse.text: Dear Wosli , what a great review ! We are thrilled you enjoyed your Safari with us and very grateful for you taking the time to review us. Mr T is a great guy and guests love his interaction and humble ways , MP and Jacob are obviously more "behind the scenes" but just as valuable n terms of the guest's overall experience ! This time of year can be difficult for sightings but it seems they really fell in your lap which is great !

ownerResponse.text: Thank you so much Karleen for the Lekker review , much appreciated we do hope you travel with us again someday !

title: 2 nights 3 days in the heart of Chobe National Park
text: Day 1 started with a river game viewing safari on the majestic Chobe river. At first I was excited by a few water birds, but after an hour's journey up the river into the Chobe national park, there were elephants as far as the eye could see. Hundreds of elephants! Hippos, buffalo, nile crocodiles, monitor lizards, impalas in every direction. After the river safari we were taken by game drive vehicle to the min Chobe park entrance. We had our 2nd safari of the day and saw every other animal you can think of over the 5 hour trip. Over the course of the next 2 days we did another 4 big game drives that were just more and more spectacular each time. The list is long, but we saw lions with tiny cubs, a massive herd of sable antelope, zebras, jackals, giraffes, baboons, mongooses, blue wildebeest, buffalo, impalas, kudus, warthogs - you name it, we saw it. Our magnificent guide and animal spotter par-excellence was Mr T.  So knowledgeable about every type of bird and animal. We learnt so much from him. The tented accommodation was perfect and comfortable. It's so wonderful to hear the sounds of Africa at night and wake up to the natural alarm clock of bird song. The chefs are master-chefs! Every meal was a culinary sensation, with freshly baked hot bread with every meal. I suggested that the chefs run a side-course in camp cooking and baking. I am sure there will be people interested. The whole tour was so professionally run. My husband and I had a magic time. I highly recommend Kalahari Tours. They will make your trip a dream come true.

ownerResponse.text: Dear Mayae we are thrilled you had such a good Safari with us and thank you for the lovely review! Mr T is a star and we are very lucky to have him on board our staff compliment . This time of year its not always that easy to see much game due to the rains but it seems you were really fortunate which is wonderful ! Please do come on tour with us again someday !

title: Jaw dropping (lots of hippo jaw dropping)
text: We went on a combination of land and water tours. Our guide, Six, knew the land and had a good sense of what was happening with the animals in the park. He also knew the best way to interact with the animals in a calm way that ensured both animals and humans were safe and happy. Highly recommend these tours.

ownerResponse.text: Dear Sarah , Thank you for taking the time to give us this lovely review . We will let Six know you enjoyed his services . Please do come again ....

title: Mind blowing!
text:
Wonderful safari and boat cruises - we were blown away by how much wildlife we saw. Our guide, Six, was incredible. He seemed to know where to find the animals and was very keen to ensure we got the most out of our time on tours. 
Highly recommended! Make sure you ask for Six!

ownerResponse.text: Dear Katalex , Thanks for the awesome review, we will be sure to send your commendations on to Six ! Thanks for spreading the word and do come again someday to visit us!
